rulings.Case Title: KEVIN WILLIAMS VS. ERIC SAFIRE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Vacate Order Of Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KEVIN WILLIAMS' Mtn To Vacate Order Of Dismissal Plaintiff's motion to vacate dismissal is denied. Relief on the ground of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect" must be shown to have been excusable under the circumstances under CCP § 473(b). Here, Plaintiff's neglect is not excusable. Plaintiff did nothing from March 2013 to March 2015. He did not contact Defendants, nor did he file a single document with the Court. Plaintiff was given notice of the Order to Show Cause by the Court but he did not appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/lt

rulings.Case Title: FERNANDO GONZALEZ VS. DWAIN ALAN DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants Financial Position
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday May 21, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FERNANDO GONZALEZ' Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendants Financial Position. Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. To prevail on this motion, the plaintiff must show it is very likely he will obtain a punitive damages award at trial. (Jabro v. Superior Court(2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 754.) Plaintiff has not met this very high standard at this time. The denial is without prejudice to the court's reconsideration if defendant Davis pleads guilty or is convicted of driving under the influence of alcohol. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY DEWITT VS. CRAZY PROTOCOL COMMUNICATIONS, INC. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside March 16, 2015 Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT IAC/INTERACTIVECORP's Motion To Set Aside March 16, 2015 Default Granted. The summons does not state that Defendant is sued under a fictitious name, as required by CCP sec. 474. Accordingly, the Court does not have personal jurisdiction over defendant. (Armstrong v. Sup. Ct. (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 420, 424.) The default is therefore void and set aside pursuant to CCP sec. 473(d). (See Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 852.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/lt

rulings.Case Title: ELENA JURADO et al VS. YISHUN LI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Elena Jurado And Donna Jurado'S Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday May 21, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiff Elena Jurado And Donna Jurado's Motion To Compel Further Responses (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted in part. Interrogatories 1, 4, and 6. Granted. The Court notes that the interrogatories use the present tense and don't refer to any past dates. Therefore, the properties and rental units that defendants manage or own and their policies are the properties and rental units that they have managed or owned, and the policies that they have had, at any time from the date the interrogatories were served through the date defendants answer the interrogatories. Interrogatory 7. Granted in part. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. Defendants need only respond as to: (1) complaints relating to matters alleged in the complaint, i.e.., water intrusion, the electrical system, vermin, wiring, the heat, hazardous fluids or materials, weatherproofing, evictions, and retaliation; and (2) complaints filed with the court or a government agency that are a public record that relate to other things. In answering, defendants need to make reasonable inquiry of Gaetani and also read any discovery responses provided by Gaetani on this subject. Interrogatories 13 and 14. Granted in part. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. Defendants need only respond as to repairs relating to matters alleged in the complaint, i.e.., water intrusion, the electrical system, vermin, wiring, the heat, hazardous fluids or materials, and weatherproofing. In answering, defendants need to make reasonable inquiry of Gaetani and also read any discovery responses provided by Gaetani on these subjects. Interrogatory 17. Granted. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. In answering, defendants need to make reasonable inquiry of Gaetani and also read any discovery responses provided by Gaetani on this subject. Interrogatories 31, 32, and 34. Granted. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. Document Request 13. Granted. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. (continued on part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELENA JURADO et al VS. YISHUN LI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Elena Jurado And Donna Jurado'S Motion To Compel Further Responses (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday May 21, 2015, Line 3, Plaintiff Elena Jurado And Donna Jurado's Motion To Compel Further Responses (part 2 of 2). Document Request 14. Granted in part. The "subject property" is as plaintiffs define it, i.e., all or any part of the property at 2195 42nd Avenue, San Francisco. Defendants need only produce communications concerning or relating to the sale that relate to: (1) the condition of the property, including without limitation, water intrusion, the electrical system, vermin, wiring, the heat, hazardous fluids or materials, and weatherproofing; or (2) complaints filed with the court or a government agency that are a public record that relate to other things. Document Request 19: Granted. A privilege log shall be provided as to all documents withheld on grounds of privilege other than documents written solely by either defendants or their present attorneys and sent only to defendants or their present attorneys. June 22, 2015 shall be the deadline for serving the privilege log, the further verified responses to interrogatories and document requests required by this order, and all documents required to be produced by this order. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION VS. LAW OFFICES OF BORNSTEIN & BORNSTEIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sancations For Attorneys Fees Or For Alternative Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF FIRST MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Terminating Sancations For Attorneys Fees Or For Alternative Sanctions The Motion for Terminating Sanctions is DENIED. The Motion for Issue Sanctions and/or Evidentiary Sanctions is GRANTED. First Mercury shall prepare an order seeking issue sanctions as they relate to the unanswered interrogatories, and evidence sanctions as they relate to the documents sought but not produced under the Jan. 2015 Order. The request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
